name: snooze-from-held-state-resnoozes-without-error
description: A row already in state='held' (previously snoozed) must accept a fresh handleSnooze without crashing or leaving state inconsistent. Common flow: user snoozes 1h, then wants to push it to tomorrow — re-snoozing must just overwrite snoozed_until. Regression guard for any state-machine check that rejects held → held transitions.

db.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM tracked_items WHERE id = $fixture:0 AND state = 'held'
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM tracked_items WHERE id = $fixture:0 AND CAST(json_extract(metadata,'$.snoozed_until') AS INTEGER) > strftime('%s','now')*1000 + 5*3600*1000
